title: Support non-overridable parameters in classes
body:
SystemVerilog allows `parameter` and `localparam` to declare constants
within a class scope.  E.g.

```SystemVerilog
class C;
  localparam A = 10;
endclass
```

In this context both declare a local parameter that can not be overwritten.

Parameters that have been declared in a class scope can also be accessed
via a object of the class type. E.g.

```SystemVerilog
class C;
  localparam A = 10;
endclass

initial begin
  C c = new
  $display(c.A);
end
```

Supporting this requires a bit of refactoring of the parameter elaboration
code to work with a const NetScope* since the class_type_t only allows
const access to the scope.
